description: Fix all multi-instance feature gaps (port-aware match patterns, fragile per-tab fallback, duplicate hostname detection, tabId+instance precedence) and add extensive E2E stress tests to make multi-instance plugin support ruthlessly robust for self-hosted services like Grafana, Retool, and SQLPad running on different ports of the same host.

userStories.title: Port-aware match pattern derivation in settings resolver
userStories.description: Change deriveMatchPattern to include the port in Chrome match patterns when the URL has a non-standard port (not 80/443/empty). Currently it produces *://hostname/* which makes two instances on localhost:3000 and localhost:3001 indistinguishable.
acceptanceCriteria: deriveMatchPattern('http://localhost:3000/app') returns '*://localhost:3000/*'
deriveMatchPattern('https://grafana.example.com/dashboard') returns '*://grafana.example.com/*' (standard HTTPS port omitted)
deriveMatchPattern('http://grafana.example.com') returns '*://grafana.example.com/*' (standard HTTP port omitted)
deriveMatchPattern('http://localhost:8080/foo?bar=1') returns '*://localhost:8080/*'
Existing unit tests in settings-resolver.test.ts updated to reflect port-aware patterns
New unit tests cover port variations (explicit 80, explicit 443, non-standard ports, no port)

userStories.title: Port-aware hostname matching in findTabForInstance and plugin_list_tabs
userStories.description: Update hostnameFromPattern in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts and plugin-list-tabs.ts to extract host:port (not just hostname) from match patterns, and update findTabForInstance to compare host:port against tab URLs. Same for annotateTabsWithInstance in plugin-list-tabs.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: hostnameFromPattern('*://localhost:3000/*') returns 'localhost:3000'
hostnameFromPattern('*://example.com/*') returns 'example.com'
findTabForInstance correctly matches tab at localhost:3000 to pattern *://localhost:3000/* and does NOT match tab at localhost:3001
annotateTabsWithInstance in plugin-list-tabs.ts correctly labels tabs with port-specific instances
Existing unit tests in mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts and plugin-list-tabs.test.ts updated

userStories.title: Eliminate fragile fallback in resolvePerTabSettings
userStories.description: When no instance matches a tab's URL in resolvePerTabSettings, the current code falls back to Object.values(urlMap)[0] which silently returns the wrong instance's URL. Change to return an empty string instead of a random instance's URL, and log a warning.
acceptanceCriteria: When a tab's URL does not match any instance in the instanceMap, url-type fields resolve to empty string ''
A console.warn is logged identifying the unmatched tab URL and plugin name
The fallback to Object.values(urlMap)[0] is removed

userStories.title: Detect and warn on duplicate derived patterns in settings resolver
userStories.description: When two instances produce the same Chrome match pattern (e.g., same hostname and port), log a warning during settings resolution. This helps users catch misconfiguration early.
acceptanceCriteria: When two instance entries derive the same match pattern, a warning is logged naming both instances and the duplicate pattern
The second instance still lands in the instanceMap (last-write-wins, as before) — no behavioral change, just a warning
Unit test verifies warning is logged for duplicate patterns

userStories.title: Clear tabId vs instance precedence with warning
userStories.description: When both tabId and instance are provided in a tool call, tabId should take precedence (it's more specific). Currently instance takes precedence (the if-block runs first). Swap the precedence: if tabId is provided, use it and log a warning that instance is being ignored.
acceptanceCriteria: When both tabId and instance are provided, tabId is used for dispatch
A warning is logged: 'Both tabId and instance provided for tool X — using tabId, ignoring instance'
When only instance is provided, behavior is unchanged
When only tabId is provided, behavior is unchanged

userStories.title: E2E test: same-host-different-port dispatch
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that two instances on the same hostname but different ports (e.g., localhost:PORT_A and localhost:PORT_B) are correctly distinguished — dispatch to instance alpha hits server A, dispatch to instance beta hits server B.
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts two test servers on ephemeral ports, both on localhost
Config uses both as instances of the same plugin (alpha: localhost:PORT_A, beta: localhost:PORT_B)
Dispatching with instance 'alpha' hits only server A
Dispatching with instance 'beta' hits only server B
plugin_list_tabs shows both tabs with correct instance labels
getConfig('instanceUrl') returns the correct port-specific URL in each tab
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (NEW file — all new E2E tests from US-006 through US-016 go in this file)

Context:
This is THE critical real-world test — self-hosted services on localhost with different ports is the most common multi-instance scenario. The existing multi-instance.e2e.ts tests use localhost vs 127.0.0.1 (different hostnames), which dodged the port issue.

Use the same test infrastructure pattern as e2e/multi-instance.e2e.ts:
- `startTestServer()` to get two servers on ephemeral ports (both will be localhost)
- `writeTestConfig` with settings that map alpha and beta to the two localhost URLs WITH different ports
- `openTestAppTab` to open tabs for each
- `waitForReadyTabs` to wait for adapter injection
- `client.callTool` to dispatch

Key difference from existing tests: both servers are on localhost, distinguished only by port. The config should look like:
```ts
settings: {
  'e2e-test': {
    instanceUrl: {
      alpha: `http://localhost:${alphaPort}`,
      beta: `http://localhost:${betaPort}`,
    },
  },
},
```

After US-001 and US-002, this should work because patterns will be `*://localhost:PORT_A/*` and `*://localhost:PORT_B/*`.

IMPORTANT: Create a shared setup/teardown helper at the top of the new file (similar to `setupMultiInstanceTest` in multi-instance.e2e.ts) that can be parameterized for different test scenarios. Many tests in this file will need the same infrastructure.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify existing multi-instance.e2e.ts — these are additional tests in a NEW file
- Follow the same test patterns as existing E2E tests (test.slow(), try/finally cleanup)
- Use test.describe blocks for grouping

userStories.title: E2E test: single-instance seamless (no instance parameter needed)
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that a single-instance configuration works without requiring the instance parameter in tool calls — the tool schema should not include an instance enum, and dispatch should work without passing instance.
acceptanceCriteria: Config has a single instance (e.g., instanceUrl: { default: URL })
Tool schema does NOT include an 'instance' property
Tool schema does NOT require 'instance'
Dispatch without passing instance succeeds and hits the correct server
getConfig('instanceUrl') returns the single URL as a string
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file from US-006)

Context:
The schema injection logic in mcp-setup.ts (lines 363-373) only adds the `instance` parameter when `instanceNames.length >= 2`. With a single instance, there should be no `instance` parameter at all, and dispatch should auto-select the only matching tab.

Test steps:
1. Start one test server
2. Write config with single instance: `{ instanceUrl: { default: serverUrl } }`
3. Start MCP server + extension
4. Open one tab to the test server
5. List tools — verify e2e-test_echo does NOT have `instance` in its schema
6. Call e2e-test_echo without `instance` parameter — verify it succeeds
7. Call e2e-test_sdk_get_config for 'instanceUrl' — verify it returns the URL string

Constraints:
- The e2e-test plugin has static urlPatterns: ['http://localhost/*'] — for single-instance with a specific port, the derived pattern will be `*://localhost:PORT/*` which should ALSO match. But the static pattern `http://localhost/*` might also match. This is fine — both patterns matching the same tab is harmless.
- Do NOT modify the e2e-test plugin's package.json

userStories.title: E2E test: hot-reload instance addition and removal
userStories.description: Add E2E tests verifying that adding or removing instances via config.json + POST /reload correctly updates the plugin's instance routing without server restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Test 1 (addition): starts with 2 instances (alpha, beta), modifies config to add gamma, triggers POST /reload, opens gamma tab, verifies dispatch to gamma works
Test 2 (removal): starts with 2 instances (alpha, beta), modifies config to remove beta, triggers POST /reload, verifies dispatch to beta returns 'Unknown instance' error
Tool schema updates after reload (instance enum reflects the new set of instances)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
POST /reload triggers `performConfigReload` → `reloadCore` which re-runs settings resolution and rebuilds the registry. The existing E2E patterns for POST /reload can be found in e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts and e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts.

The reload helper pattern:
```ts
const triggerReload = async (port: number, secret: string) => {
  const headers = { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` };
  return fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers });
};
```

For the addition test:
1. Setup with alpha + beta (two servers, two instances)
2. Start a third test server (gamma)
3. Rewrite config.json adding gamma instance
4. POST /reload
5. Wait for tools/list_changed (re-list tools to verify instance enum now has 3 values)
6. Open gamma tab
7. Wait for ready
8. Dispatch to gamma — verify it hits the gamma server

For the removal test:
1. Setup with alpha + beta
2. Rewrite config.json removing beta
3. POST /reload
4. Re-list tools — verify instance enum now only has alpha
5. Dispatch to beta — verify 'Unknown instance' error

Use `writeTestConfig` to rewrite the config file, then POST /reload. After reload, use `client.listTools()` to verify the schema changed, and `client.callTool` to verify dispatch.

Constraints:
- Use the same configDir from initial setup (overwrite config.json in place)
- Wait for the reload to complete before making assertions (check tools/list_changed or poll listTools)

userStories.title: E2E test: tabId + instance conflict precedence
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that when both tabId and instance are provided and they disagree, tabId takes precedence.
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch with instance='alpha' and tabId pointing to the beta tab executes in the beta tab (tabId wins)
The tool call succeeds (no error)
The beta server receives the call, not the alpha server
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
After US-005, tabId takes precedence over instance. This test verifies that.

Test steps:
1. Setup with alpha (localhost:PORT_A) and beta (use 127.0.0.1:PORT_B to make it easily distinguishable)
2. Open both tabs, wait for ready
3. Get the beta tab's tabId from plugin_list_tabs
4. Call e2e-test_echo with instance='alpha' AND tabId=<beta_tab_id>
5. Reset both servers' invocation counters before the call
6. Verify beta server received the call (tabId won), alpha server did not

Note: The dispatch with a mismatched tabId+instance should still succeed because tabId is a valid tab for the plugin — the security check validates the tab URL matches the plugin's URL patterns (which include both instances).

Constraints:
- Use different hostnames (localhost vs 127.0.0.1) to make the distinction clear in assertions
- The tabId must be a valid tab for the plugin (just pointing to a different instance)

userStories.title: E2E stress test: rapid sequential dispatch across instances
userStories.description: Add E2E stress test firing 20 rapid tool calls alternating between instances, verifying zero cross-routing under load.
acceptanceCriteria: 20 sequential calls alternate between alpha and beta instances
Each call returns the correct instance's response
Alpha server receives exactly 10 calls, beta server receives exactly 10 calls
No call routes to the wrong instance
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
The existing multi-instance.e2e.ts has a concurrent dispatch test (Test 6) using Promise.all with 2 calls. This stress test goes further with 20 sequential calls to exercise the dispatch path under sustained load.

Test steps:
1. Setup with alpha + beta (use localhost vs 127.0.0.1 for easy distinction)
2. Open both tabs, wait for ready
3. Reset invocation counters
4. Fire 20 sequential echo calls: even indices go to alpha, odd to beta
5. Each call includes a unique message (e.g., `call-${i}-alpha` or `call-${i}-beta`)
6. Verify each call's response matches the expected message
7. Check invocation counts: alpha=10, beta=10
8. Optionally verify the message content of each invocation on each server

Constraints:
- Sequential (not concurrent) — this tests sustained throughput, not parallel handling
- Use test.slow() as this will take time
- Include a timeout appropriate for 20 round-trips

userStories.title: E2E stress test: concurrent burst dispatch across instances
userStories.description: Add E2E stress test firing 10 concurrent tool calls (5 to each instance) via Promise.all, verifying correct routing under parallel load.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 concurrent calls dispatched via Promise.all — 5 to alpha, 5 to beta
All 10 calls succeed without error
Alpha server receives exactly 5 calls, beta server receives exactly 5 calls
Each call's response matches its expected instance
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
The existing multi-instance.e2e.ts has a concurrent test with only 2 calls. This stress test uses 10 concurrent calls to exercise the dispatch pipeline's concurrency handling.

Test steps:
1. Setup with alpha + beta
2. Open both tabs, wait for ready
3. Reset invocation counters
4. Build array of 10 promises: indices 0-4 → alpha, indices 5-9 → beta
5. Each call has a unique message: `burst-${i}`
6. Promise.all the 10 calls
7. Verify all 10 results are non-error
8. Verify alpha got 5 calls, beta got 5 calls

Constraints:
- The MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN is 25 in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts, so 10 per plugin (5 per instance) is well within limits
- Use test.slow()

userStories.title: E2E test: instance tab close and reopen recovery
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that closing an instance's tab causes dispatch to fail with a clear error, and reopening the tab restores dispatch capability.
acceptanceCriteria: With both tabs open, dispatch to both instances works
After closing beta's tab, dispatch to beta returns 'No open tab' error
Dispatch to alpha still works while beta is closed
After reopening beta's tab, dispatch to beta works again
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
This tests the full lifecycle: open → close → error → reopen → recover. The existing multi-instance.e2e.ts Test 7 only tests the missing-tab case (never opened). This tests the close+reopen cycle.

Test steps:
1. Setup with alpha + beta (localhost vs 127.0.0.1)
2. Open both tabs, wait for 2 ready tabs
3. Verify dispatch to both works
4. Close beta tab (page.close())
5. Wait for tab mapping to update (poll plugin_list_tabs until beta tab disappears or becomes unavailable)
6. Dispatch to beta → expect 'No open tab' error
7. Dispatch to alpha → expect success (isolated, not affected)
8. Reopen beta tab (new page, navigate to beta URL)
9. Wait for beta tab to become ready
10. Dispatch to beta → expect success

Constraints:
- After closing a tab, the extension's tab state needs time to update. Poll plugin_list_tabs or use a waitFor helper.
- The tab removal is detected by the extension's chrome.tabs.onRemoved listener

userStories.title: E2E test: all instance tabs closed returns appropriate error
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that when all instance tabs are closed, dispatch returns a clear error and plugin_list_tabs shows the plugin in 'closed' state.
acceptanceCriteria: With both tabs closed, dispatch to alpha returns 'No open tab' error
With both tabs closed, dispatch to beta returns 'No open tab' error
plugin_list_tabs shows the plugin with state 'closed' and empty tabs array
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
Test steps:
1. Setup with alpha + beta
2. Open both tabs, wait for ready
3. Close both tabs
4. Wait for tab mapping to reflect closed state (poll plugin_list_tabs until tabs array is empty or state is 'closed')
5. Dispatch to alpha → expect 'No open tab' error
6. Dispatch to beta → expect 'No open tab' error
7. Call plugin_list_tabs → verify state is 'closed' and tabs is empty or absent

Constraints:
- After closing tabs, the extension needs time to process onRemoved events. Use a waitFor with polling.

userStories.title: E2E test: URL path and trailing slash variations in pattern derivation
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that URLs with paths, trailing slashes, and query parameters all derive correct match patterns and route correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Instance URL 'http://localhost:PORT/grafana/' and tab opened to 'http://localhost:PORT/grafana/dashboards' match correctly
Dispatch to the instance works
getConfig returns the original URL with path
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
The pattern derivation strips paths: `http://localhost:3000/grafana/` → `*://localhost:3000/*`. This means the pattern matches ALL paths on that host:port, not just /grafana/. This is intentional — Chrome match patterns don't support path prefixes in a useful way for adapter injection (the adapter needs to run on all pages of the instance).

This test verifies that the pattern still matches correctly when:
- The config URL has a path (e.g., http://localhost:PORT/app/)
- The tab URL has a different path (e.g., http://localhost:PORT/app/dashboard)
- The match still works because the pattern is host:port-based

Test steps:
1. Start one test server
2. Config: `instanceUrl: { main: 'http://localhost:PORT/grafana/' }`
3. Open tab to `http://localhost:PORT/grafana/dashboards` (or just the root, since test server serves all paths)
4. Wait for ready
5. Dispatch echo → verify success
6. getConfig('instanceUrl') → verify returns `'http://localhost:PORT/grafana/'` (the original config URL, not the pattern)

Constraints:
- The test server serves all paths, so the specific path doesn't matter for the response
- This test uses a single instance, so no instance parameter needed

userStories.title: E2E stress test: per-tab getConfig isolation under concurrent load
userStories.description: Add E2E stress test firing concurrent getConfig calls to both instances simultaneously, verifying no cross-contamination of per-tab settings.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 concurrent getConfig calls — 5 to alpha instance, 5 to beta instance
All alpha calls return the alpha URL
All beta calls return the beta URL
No cross-contamination between instances
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
This verifies that resolvePerTabSettings correctly isolates settings per tab even under concurrent dispatch. The concern: if the resolution has any shared mutable state, concurrent calls might cross-contaminate.

Test steps:
1. Setup with alpha + beta (use localhost vs 127.0.0.1)
2. Open both tabs, wait for ready
3. Build 10 concurrent getConfig calls via Promise.all:
   - Indices 0-4: `e2e-test_sdk_get_config({ key: 'instanceUrl', instance: 'alpha' })`
   - Indices 5-9: `e2e-test_sdk_get_config({ key: 'instanceUrl', instance: 'beta' })`
4. Verify all alpha results return the alpha URL
5. Verify all beta results return the beta URL

Constraints:
- resolvePerTabSettings creates a new object each time (no shared state), so this should pass — but it's the kind of thing that must be tested under load to be sure

userStories.title: E2E test: duplicate hostname warning is logged
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that when two instances resolve to the same match pattern, the MCP server logs a warning about ambiguous routing.
acceptanceCriteria: Config has two instances with URLs that derive the same pattern (e.g., same host:port with different paths)
Server logs contain a warning about ambiguous instance routing
Dispatch still works (last-write-wins behavior, no crash)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance-robust.e2e.ts (append to the new file)

Context:
After US-004, the settings resolver logs a warning when two instances produce the same pattern. This test verifies the warning appears in the MCP server's output.

Test steps:
1. Start one test server on PORT_A
2. Config with two instances that resolve to the same host:port:
   ```
   instanceUrl: {
     prod: `http://localhost:${PORT_A}/prod`,
     staging: `http://localhost:${PORT_A}/staging`,
   }
   ```
   Both derive `*://localhost:PORT_A/*` — same pattern.
3. Start MCP server
4. Check server stdout/stderr for the warning string: 'instance routing will be ambiguous'
5. Open one tab to the test server
6. Dispatch should still work (the last-write-wins instance gets the tab)

To check server logs, the MCP server fixture captures stdout. Use `server.stdout` or grep the server output for the warning.

Constraints:
- This is the FINAL test in the file and has e2eCheckpoint: true — ralph will run the full E2E suite after this story
- The warning is logged by the MCP server (platform/mcp-server), not the extension
- Check the MCP server fixture's output capturing mechanism — look at how other tests check server logs (e.g., `waitForLog` helper in e2e/helpers.ts)
